code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test25-APR-2025Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Serum creatinine is elevated, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis shows significant proteinuria, glucosuria, mild ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), with low specific gravity. No evidence of infection (negative nitrites, leukocytes). Findings are suggestive of possible glomerular dysfunction or early diabetic nephropathy; correlation with clinical context and further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
